title: Intensely Smoky Greek Lamb Skewers
description: A charred and flavorful Greek dish featuring lamb skewers with olives and a tangy ferment jang sauce, perfect for a brunch mood, halal friendly and full of cheese pull goodness.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Grill the skewers for 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are charred and cooked through.
While the lamb is cooking, mix the ferment jang sauce and lemon juice in a small bowl.
Once the lamb is done, brush the skewers with the ferment jang sauce mixture.
Serve the skewers hot, topped with crumbly feta cheese, green olives, and a side of warm pita bread.
Garnish with fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice, if desired.
